mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate above 1.5% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through direct repricing of future cash flows as cost of capital rises.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 1.5% and continues rising for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.77% on 2022-05-01 with Fed in early tightening cycle
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rate hikes accelerating
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises above 1.5% for 2+ consecutive months, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate declining below 3.8% signals tight labor market and wage pressure that forces corporate margin compression, reducing earnings growth expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices profit forecasts downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6% on 2022-05-01, already below historical median
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of months where UNRATE < 3.8%, or correlation between UNRATE and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) declining month-over-month by more than 2% signals weakening consumer demand and recession risk, triggering equity risk-off positioning that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days as portfolio managers reduce equity exposure in anticipation of earnings downgrades.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 672874M on 2022-05-01 with recent volatility
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 58% of instances following a >2% monthly RSAFS decline, or the average 5-day return following such declines is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerating below 0.5% month-over-month combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 8% year-over-year) signals stagflation conditions where supply constraints prevent demand accommodation, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as stagflation reduces real earnings and equity risk premiums expand.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation > 8%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 291.474 on 2022-05-01 implying ~8.6% YoY inflation
regime_hint: stagflation regime with supply constraints and elevated inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months meeting both trigger conditions, or the interaction effect (joint condition) does not explain incremental variance beyond single-series effects (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1600K units annualized signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand weakness, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as housing is a leading indicator of broader economic activity and equity valuations compress on recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1724K on 2022-04-01, near trigger threshold
regime_hint: demand-side deceleration with housing market weakness
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 62% of months where HOUST < 1600K, or the predictive power of HOUST for 60-day returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating to below 100K new jobs per month signals labor market deterioration and forward earnings risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations and risk premiums widen on recession concerns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth falls below 100K jobs
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151682K on 2022-05-01 with strong recent growth
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of months where PAYEMS growth < 100K, or the correlation between PAYEMS growth and subsequent 20-day returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth exceeding 8% combined with federal funds rate remaining below 2% signals monetary policy lag where real rates are deeply negative, creating asset price inflation that eventually corrects sharply, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as the market anticipates aggressive Fed tightening and valuation compression.
regime_hint: monetary policy lag regime with negative real rates and high inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances where both conditions are met, or the interaction term does not provide incremental explanatory power beyond individual series (R² improvement < 0.015).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating to below 1.5% annualized signals economic slowdown and earnings recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity valuations compress on lower growth expectations and increased recession probability.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19731.119B on 2022-01-01 with recent revision to 21932.71B indicating data volatility
regime_hint: economic slowdown or early recession regime
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth < 1.5%, or the predictive relationship between GDP growth and 60-day equity returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).
